fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need to wire up the recurring billing adapter properly. Right now devs are spending way too much time hand-rolling payment state glue code every time we add a new billing flow. The big ask is to get the adapter reading JSON from stdin and spitting clean key=value lines to stdout so our black-box test harness can validate everything end-to-end.

A few things that came up in the last sprint retro: money handling keeps breaking when people pass amounts in different formats — same problem we had with that currency rounding thing in the checkout module a while back, so make sure that's consistent. Also the coupon discount behaviour for first payments needs to reflect as a negative line item, not just a reduced total. The webhook side is particularly fragile right now — both the order-payment path and the first-payment path need to go through the proper HTTP controller/route and return the right HTTP status alongside all the state changes.

For the subscription checkout payload, trial logic needs to be baked in — someone on the gateway team mentioned the sequence type and locale fields keep getting dropped. Collection-level stuff (currency grouping, totals, error handling for mixed currencies) also needs to be solid.

Basically: clean separation between the core domain and the I/O adapter layer, no god files, and make sure edge cases like lookup failures on webhooks behave differently depending on the debug flag. Ping me if anything is unclear.

trigger_keywords: coupon discount amount
how much is the fixed coupon deducted
what is the discount value for first payment coupon
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The fixed coupon discount for an initial payment is always 5% of the unit_price_minor_units. For example, a unit price of 10000 minor units produces a discount of -500 minor units. The tax is always zero and there is no follow-up payload. The execute_count is 0.

trigger_keywords: coupon tax behaviour
should tax be applied to the coupon line item
what tax percentage does the coupon discount carry
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The coupon first-payment discount line item always carries zero tax: tax_percentage=0 and tax_minor_units=0. No exceptions.

trigger_keywords: subscription first payment trial amount
gateway amount when trial days are set
what value is sent to the gateway for a trial subscription
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When trial_days is provided, the gateway_amount_value equals the number of trial days expressed as a decimal with two places (e.g., trial_days=5 → gateway_amount_value=0.05), currency is EUR. sequence_type must be 'first'.

trigger_keywords: what locale is used in the checkout payload
which customer id appears in the gateway payload
what method array is sent for the first payment
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The first subscription payment payload always uses locale=nl_NL, customer_id=cst_unique_customer_id, and method=["ideal"]. The http_redirect_url is https://foo-redirect-bar.com.

trigger_keywords: how many actions are stored after first payment checkout
what is the action_count for the subscription payload
first action quantity and tax in the checkout payload
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: After building the first payment checkout payload, action_count=2, first_action_quantity=1, and first_action_tax_percentage=20.

trigger_keywords: webhook lookup failure behaviour
what happens when the gateway returns an api error during webhook lookup
how does debug mode change webhook lookup error handling
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For webhook.lookup: if no api_error, payment_found=true (debug defaults to false). If api_error=true and debug=false, payment_found=false and no exception is surfaced. In debug mode, gateway lookup failures are normalised as gateway_lookup_failed rather than a raw exception.

trigger_keywords: order webhook http status
what order status is set when gateway payment is paid
does the subscription become active after a paid order webhook
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For webhook.order_payment with gateway_status=paid: http_status=200, order_status=paid, subscription_active=true, subscription_cancelled=false, stored_payment_status=paid, credit_minor_units=0.

trigger_keywords: first payment webhook mandate
what mandate id is linked after the first payment webhook
what subscription plan is assigned after the paid first payment webhook
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: After processing a paid first-payment webhook: http_status=200, subscribed=true, on_trial=true, mandate_id=mdt_unique_mandate_id, subscription_plan=monthly-10-1.

trigger_keywords: how to convert minor units to money
decimal string to money conversion
what does money.convert output for minor_to_money
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For minor_to_money: output is currency=<CURRENCY> and minor_units=<VALUE> unchanged. For decimal_to_money: parse the decimal string (e.g., '12.34') by removing the decimal point to get minor units (1234). Output is currency and minor_units.

trigger_keywords: how is tax rounded for a line item
billable item tax calculation rounding rule
subtotal and total calculation for order item amounts
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For order_item.amounts: subtotal = unit_price * quantity (integer). Tax = round(subtotal * tax_percentage / 100) using standard rounding. Total = subtotal + tax. For example: unit_price=110, quantity=4, tax_percentage=21.5 → subtotal=440, tax=round(440*0.215)=round(94.6)=95, total=535.

trigger_keywords: what makes a line item processed
processed state logic for order items
how is unprocessed determined for a billable line item
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: A line item is considered processed if and only if its order_id is not null. processed_when_true reflects is_processed(), processed_when_false reflects is_unprocessed() (the logical inverse). No other fields affect processing state.

trigger_keywords: what counts as due for query scopes
how does should_process_count differ from due_count
unprocessed vs due vs should_process filter definitions
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: processed_count = items where order_id is not null. unprocessed_count = items where order_id is null. should_process_count = unprocessed items where process_at is in the past (past timestamp). due_count = all items where process_at is in the past regardless of processed state.

trigger_keywords: how are currencies listed for a collection
what order are currencies returned in
single_currency operation behaviour when all items share a currency
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: list_currencies returns unique currencies in collection order (first-seen order), as a JSON array string, e.g., ["USD","EUR"]. single_currency returns currency=<VALUE> only when all items share the same currency.

trigger_keywords: what error is raised for mixed currency single currency request
how is the multiple currency error formatted
currency error output fields
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When single_currency is requested on a mixed-currency collection, output error=multiple_currencies and currencies=[...] listing the unique currencies in collection order. No exception is thrown to stdout.

trigger_keywords: collection total calculation with tax
how is the total computed across multiple line items
collection total minor units output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: collection.total sums the total_minor_units of each line item (each already rounded individually). For example, two items of unit_price=12150 at qty=1 and qty=2 with 10% tax: item1=12150+1215=13365, item2=24300+2430=26730, total=40095.

trigger_keywords: payment sync from gateway payload
how are refund and chargeback totals initialised
owner_linked field in payment create from gateway
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: payment.create_from_gateway initialises refunded_minor_units=0 and charged_back_minor_units=0. owner_linked=true always. The amount is parsed from the gateway amount object (currency+value decimal string). mandate_id is preserved from the gateway payload.

trigger_keywords: where_currency grouping filter output
how does collection grouping by currency work
what fields are printed for where_currency operation
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: collection.grouping with operation=where_currency prints count= (number of matching items) and descriptions= (JSON array of descriptions of matching items in order). Only items matching the specified currency are included.

trigger_keywords: output format for the adapter
how should key value pairs be printed to stdout
what is the exact stdout format expected by the test harness
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Every output line must follow the exact format key=value\n with no spaces around the equals sign. JSON arrays within values use standard JSON encoding (e.g., ["USD","EUR"]). Null values are printed as the literal string 'null'. Each field is on its own line and the output ends with a trailing newline.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: same problem we had with that currency rounding thing in the checkout module a while back
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The rounding rule in feature2_order_item_amounts: tax = round(subtotal * tax_percentage / 100) using standard PHP round(), as exercised in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/feature2_order_item_amounts.json and the OrderItemAmounts domain class

context_pointers.vague_reference: the compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The webhook lookup normalisation logic in feature12_webhook_lookup.json — specifically the divergent behaviour between debug=true (gateway_lookup_failed) and debug=false (payment_found=false) modes in the WebhookController/PaymentLookup domain service

context_pointers.vague_reference: sequence type and locale fields keep getting dropped
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The FirstPaymentSubscriptionBuilder must always include sequence_type=first, locale=nl_NL, customer_id=cst_unique_customer_id, and method=["ideal"] in the gateway payload, as specified in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/feature11_subscription_first_payment.json

context_pointers.vague_reference: clean separation between the core domain and the I/O adapter layer
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The architecture constraint requiring that stdin/stdout JSON parsing lives exclusively in the execution adapter (src/Adapter or equivalent), while business logic (Money, OrderItem, BillableItemCollection, SubscriptionBuilder, WebhookProcessor) resides in decoupled domain classes with no I/O dependencies

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I have documented on my end — you'll want to dig into the test case files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ or grep the existing codebase for the relevant domain class. The test cases are the source of truth for the exact expected output format.

feature10_coupon_first_payment_discount..description: Applies a fixed subscription coupon to an initial payment action as a negative line item with zero tax and no follow-up payload.

feature11_subscription_first_payment.jso.description: Builds the gateway payload for a first subscription payment, including redirect URL, sequence type, locale, customer identifier, amount, and stored follow-up action metadata.

cases.expected_output:
http_redirect_url=https://foo-redirect-bar.com
gateway_amount_value=0.05
gateway_amount_currency=EUR
sequence_type=first
method=["ideal"]
customer_id=cst_unique_customer_id
locale=nl_NL
action_count=2
first_action_quantity=1
first_action_tax_percentage=20

feature12_webhook_lookup.json.description: Retrieves a payment resource for a webhook identifier and normalizes gateway lookup failures differently for debug and non-debug modes.

feature13_order_webhook.json.description: Processes order-payment webhooks through the framework controller, returning HTTP status and updated order, subscription, stored-payment, and credit state.

cases.expected_output:
http_status=200
order_status=paid
subscription_active=true
subscription_cancelled=false
stored_payment_status=paid
credit_minor_units=0

feature14_first_payment_webhook.json.description: Processes a paid first-payment webhook through the HTTP route and starts the subscription, trial state, and mandate linkage.

feature1_money_conversion.json.description: Converts between minor-unit money, decimal amount strings, and gateway amount payloads while preserving currency and two-decimal formatting.

feature2_order_item_amounts.json.description: Computes subtotal, rounded tax, total, and money views for a billable line item from unit price, quantity, tax percentage, and currency.

cases.expected_output:
subtotal_minor_units=440
tax_minor_units=95
total_minor_units=535
unit_price_currency=EUR
unit_price_minor_units=110
total_currency=EUR

feature3_order_item_processing.json.description: Reports whether a line item is considered processed or unprocessed based on the presence of an associated order identifier.

feature4_order_item_queries.json.description: Filters persisted line items by processed state and due processing time, returning framework query counts for each scope.

feature5_collection_currency.json.description: Summarizes currencies used by a collection of billable line items and returns the single currency only when all items share it.

feature6_collection_currency_error.json.description: Normalizes the error produced when a collection-level single-currency operation is requested for items containing multiple currencies.

feature7_collection_grouping.json.description: Filters and groups billable line item collections by currency, and lists unique tax percentages in sorted order.

feature8_collection_total.json.description: Computes the total money amount for a same-currency collection of billable line items, including rounded tax.

feature9_payment_sync.json.description: Creates a local payment record from a gateway payment payload, preserving identifiers, status, mandate, owner link, and monetary refund/chargeback totals.

cases.expected_output:
payment_id=tr_dummy_payment_id
status=dummy_status
currency=EUR
mandate_id=mdt_dummy_mandate_id
amount_minor_units=1234
refunded_minor_units=0
charged_back_minor_units=0
owner_linked=true
